pi_items.metadata_value: PID segment has patient demographics (ID, name, DOB, gender, etc.)
pi_items.verified_value: PID segment contains: patient ID (field 3), patient name (field 5 - contains last name and first name), DOB (field 7), gender (field 8), address (field 11)

pi_items.metadata_value: Channel XML must include a <responseTransformer> element in destination connectors
pi_items.verified_value: Response transformers are optional elements in Mirth Connect destination connectors, not mandatory
pi_items.source: Web search of Mirth Connect documentation at https://docs.nextgen.com/en-US/mirthc2ae-connect-by-nextgen-healthcare-user-guide-3273569/response-transformers-14709 shows response transformers are optional features

privileged_info_summary: The task uses NextGen Connect 4.5.0 running on PostgreSQL 15. The database is accessible at jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb with credentials postgres/postgres. The REST API is at https://localhost:8443/api with default credentials admin/admin, requiring the X-Requested-With: OpenAPI header. The sample HL7 message is an ADT A01 (admission) message in v2.3 format containing patient demographics in the PID segment (patient ID, name with last/first name, date of birth, gender). The channel should listen on TCP port 6663 and use the name 'Patient DB Writer' to write to the 'patient_records' table. However, response transformers in destination connectors are optional, not mandatory as claimed.
